_comment: DC Hub Tool Definitions — OpenAI Function Calling Format. Works with: Meta AI (Llama 3.1+), Perplexity Sonar, ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, any OpenAI-compatible agent. Paste these into your 'tools' array when making chat completion calls.

function.name: dchub_search_facilities
function.description: Search 20,000+ data center facilities worldwide by name, provider, city, or country. Returns facility name, operator, location, and coordinates. Use when a user asks about data centers in a location or by a specific operator.

query.description: Search term — matches facility name, provider, city, or region. Example: 'Equinix', 'Ashburn', 'Digital Realty'

country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code filter. Example: 'US', 'GB', 'DE', 'JP'

function.name: dchub_get_transactions
function.description: Get data center M&A transactions, acquisitions, and investments. Tracks $51B+ in total deal value. Use when a user asks about data center deals, acquisitions, mergers, or investment activity.

function.name: dchub_get_news
function.description: Get the latest data center industry news from 40+ sources, updated every 5 minutes. Use when a user asks about recent data center news, industry developments, or current events in digital infrastructure.

function.name: dchub_score_site
function.description: Score a location for data center development suitability (0-100). Evaluates energy cost, carbon intensity, infrastructure proximity, connectivity, natural disaster risk, and cost factors. Use when a user asks if a location is good for a data center or wants to compare sites.

state.description: Two-letter US state code (for energy/carbon data). Example: 'VA', 'TX', 'AZ'

function.name: dchub_get_grid_fuel_mix
function.description: Get real-time electricity generation fuel mix for a US ISO/RTO region. Returns percentage breakdown by source (gas, wind, solar, nuclear, coal, hydro). Use when a user asks about the power grid, energy sources, or electricity generation in a region.

iso.description: ISO/RTO name. ERCOT=Texas, PJM=Mid-Atlantic/Virginia, CAISO=California, MISO=Midwest, SPP=Central, NYISO=New York, ISO-NE=New England

function.name: dchub_get_energy_prices
function.description: Get electricity prices for a US state. Returns average commercial and industrial rates. Use when a user asks about power costs for data center operations in a state.

function.name: dchub_get_carbon_intensity
function.description: Get grid carbon intensity (gCO2/kWh) for a US state. Use when a user asks about the environmental impact or sustainability of data center power in a region.

function.name: dchub_compare_markets
function.description: Compare data center markets side by side. Returns capacity, pricing, growth, and key operators for each market. Use when a user asks to compare locations or markets for data center investment.

function.name: dchub_get_market_detail
function.description: Get detailed intelligence for a specific data center market, including facility count, capacity, key operators, and growth trends. Use when a user asks about a specific data center market.

function.name: dchub_get_solar_potential
function.description: Get solar PV energy potential for a location using NREL data. Returns capacity factor and annual generation estimates. Use when evaluating renewable energy options for a data center site.

function.name: dchub_get_wind_potential
function.description: Get wind energy potential for a location using NREL data. Returns capacity factor and wind speed estimates. Use when evaluating renewable energy options for a data center site.

function.name: dchub_get_construction_pipeline
function.description: Get the global data center construction pipeline. Returns facilities under construction, planned, or in development. Tracks 21+ GW of capacity. Use when a user asks about new data center builds or capacity under development.

function.name: dchub_get_gas_pipelines
function.description: Get natural gas pipeline infrastructure data by state. Useful for evaluating backup power and on-site generation options near data center sites.

function.name: dchub_get_lmp_prices
function.description: Get real-time locational marginal electricity prices across all US ISO regions. Critical for evaluating actual power costs at specific data center locations.

function.name: dchub_ask_question
function.description: Ask DC Hub a natural-language question and get a citable, structured answer with source URL. Use for any general question about data centers, markets, operators, or infrastructure that doesn't fit a specific endpoint.

question.description: Natural-language question. Example: 'How many data centers are in Northern Virginia?'

function.name: dchub_get_platform_stats
function.description: Get DC Hub platform statistics: total facilities, countries, providers, news articles, and transactions tracked. Use for high-level overview questions about the platform's coverage.

function.name: dchub_get_powered_shell_rates
function.description: Get the powered-shell lease rate band ($/sf/yr) for a data-center market. Powered shell is the long-term NNN real-estate lease hyperscalers sign before turnkey fit-out (complements dchub_get_market_intel which is $/kW/month turnkey). Returns low/mid/high band, comps backing the band, typical lease term and escalator. Markets without direct comps but with active pipeline return a triangulated estimate. Example use: 'What are powered shell rates in Northern Virginia?' or 'Greater Philadelphia lease rates?'

market.description: Market slug or display name. Examples: 'northern-virginia', 'greater-philadelphia', 'eastern-pennsylvania', 'dallas-fort-worth', 'frankfurt', 'singapore'

function.name: dchub_list_powered_shell_comps
function.description: List underlying powered-shell lease comps with deal-level detail: base rent $/sf/yr, term years, escalator %, landlord, and a link to the SEC filing or press release. Filter by market, tenant, country, or date range. Use when a user asks about specific deals like 'AWS lease comps in Virginia' or 'Microsoft powered shell deals 2025'. Requires Developer tier API key.

tenant.description: Tenant filter (aws, microsoft, meta, google, oracle, coreweave)

function.name: dchub_get_powered_shell_pipeline
function.description: List announced and under-construction powered-shell data-center sites. Covers what WILL lease (complements rate bands which cover what HAS leased). Includes AWS Pennsylvania $20B commitment, CoreWeave Lancaster, CloudHQ Berks, PAX Carlisle, QTS Prince William, Stack DFW Palmer, and other active projects. Filter by market, developer, rumored tenant, or stage.

rumored_tenant.description: Tenant if disclosed (aws, microsoft, meta, coreweave)
